title: Luis Garavito
text: Luis Garavito was a serial killer from Colombia, Ecuador, Venezuela. They were active from 1992 to 1999. The proven victim count is 138, with a possible total of 172–300+. Child-murderer, torture-killer, and rapist known as "La Bestia" ("The Beast"). Confessed to killing 140 boys between eight and 16 years old over a seven-year period in Colombia and neighboring countries. He is suspected of murdering over 300 victims, mostly street children. Garavito was originally sentenced to 1,853 years in prison, but this was later reduced to 22 years after he led police to many of the bodies of his victims. He is scheduled to become eligible for parole in 2023.

title: Pedro López
text: Pedro López was a serial killer from Colombia, Peru, Ecuador. They were active from 1969 to 1979. The proven victim count is 110, with a possible total of 300+. Child-murderer and rapist, known as "The Monster of the Andes". Targeted young girls, between the ages of eight and 12. Arrested in 1980 and convicted in 1983 of killing three young girls, but claimed to have killed hundreds. Despite being believed to be one of the most prolific serial killers of the twentieth century, he was released in the late 1990s.

title: Javed Iqbal
text: Javed Iqbal was a serial killer from Pakistan. They were active from 1998 to 1999. The proven victim count is 100, with a possible total of 100. Child-murderer and rapist, known as "Kukri", Iqbal murdered 100 street children by strangling them and covering up his crimes by dissolving the bodies with acid. He was arrested in 1999 thanks to a letter he sent to a newspaper, and was set to be executed in the manner described by the judge who stated, "You will be strangled to death in front of the parents whose children you killed, Your body will then be cut into 100 pieces and put in acid, the same way you killed the children." However, he died in custody before he could be executed.

title: Mikhail Popkov
text: Mikhail Popkov was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 1992 to 2010. The proven victim count is 83, with a possible total of 83+. Serial rapist-killer nicknamed "The Werewolf", who was active for two decades in Angarsk, Irkutsk and Vladivostok. After being convicted of 22 murders in 2015, he confessed to an additional 59 murders, of which he was convicted of 56 in 2018. In July 2020, Popkov confessed to more killings, bringing the total number of admitted victims to 83. Sentenced to life.

title: Daniel Camargo Barbosa
text: Daniel Camargo Barbosa was a serial killer from Colombia, Ecuador, Brazil (alleged). They were active from 1974 to 1986. The proven victim count is 72, with a possible total of 180. Child and woman murderer, believed to have possibly raped and killed over 150 victims, primarily targeting female children as they were more likely to be virgins. Confessed to killing 72 victims. He strangled young girls in Colombia and was arrested, but he escaped from prison and he started killing in Ecuador; rearrested in 1986, he was incarcerated in the same Ecuadorian prison as 300+ serial killer Pedro López. Camargo was killed in jail by the nephew of one of his victims.

title: Pedro Rodrigues Filho
text: Pedro Rodrigues Filho was a serial killer from Brazil. They were active from 1967 to 2003. The proven victim count is 71, with a possible total of 100+. He claimed to have killed over 100 victims, 47 of them inmates. He also killed his father and ate a piece of his heart. He killed his first two victims at the age of 14; he was first arrested in 1973. Convicted and sentenced to 128 years, but the maximum one can serve in Brazil is 30 years. Was released in 2018.

title: Kampatimar Shankariya
text: Kampatimar Shankariya was a serial killer from India. They were active from 1977 to 1978. The proven victim count is 70, with a possible total of 70+. Indian serial killer who used a hammer to kill over 70 men and women between 1978 and 1979. Was hanged in 1979, his last words were, "I have murdered in vain", he declared. "Nobody should become like me.".

title: Yang Xinhai
text: Yang Xinhai was a serial killer from China. They were active from 2000 to 2003. The proven victim count is 67, with a possible total of 67. Known as the "Monster Killer". Would enter victims' homes at night and kill using axes, meat cleavers, hammers, and shovels. Executed by gunshot in 2004.

title: Samuel Little
text: Samuel Little was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1970 to 2005. The proven victim count is 60, with a possible total of 93. Was convicted of killing three women, but later investigations linked 31 other murders to him. Little is now thought to be America's most prolific serial killer. He claimed he killed as many as 93 victims total, three above his initial confession of 90, the FBI later confirmed a total of 50 murders linked to Little. The investigations into his crimes are ongoing.

title: Giuseppe Greco
text: Giuseppe Greco was a serial killer from Italy. They were active from 1981 to 1983. The proven victim count is 58, with a possible total of 80-300. During the Second Mafia War, from 1981 to 1983 committed dozens of murders, often with his favorite weapon, an AK-47. He was eventually convicted in absentia of 58 murders, but it is believed that he committed at least 80 murders and possibly as many as 300. Murdered in his home in 1985.

title: Andrei Chikatilo
text: Andrei Chikatilo was a serial killer from Soviet Union. They were active from 1978 to 1990. The proven victim count is 53, with a possible total of 56. Known as "The Butcher of Rostov", "The Red Ripper" or "The Rostov Ripper". Convicted of the murder of 53 women and children between 1978 and 1990. One man was previously convicted and executed for his first murder. Executed by gunshot in 1994.

title: Anatoly Onoprienko
text: Anatoly Onoprienko was a serial killer from Soviet Union, Ukraine. They were active from 1989 to 1996. The proven victim count is 52, with a possible total of 52+. Known as "The Beast of Ukraine", "The Terminator" and "Citizen O". Convicted of the murders of nine people in 1989 and 43 people in 1995–1996. Traveled throughout Europe illegally from 1990 to 1995; whether he killed during this time is unknown. Sentenced to death, later commuted to imprisonment for life. Died from heart failure in 2013.

title: Ivan Maleshoff
text: Ivan Maleshoff was a serial killer from Soviet Union. They were active from 1934 to 1935. The proven victim count is 50, with a possible total of 50. Known as, "Bluebeard of Kharkiv"; Ivan Stepanovich Maleshoff was an electrical engineer and prolific albeit little known Soviet serial killer. He is purported to have killed 20 women in Kharkiv over a period of several months in 1935 with a knife, leaving behind little clues as to his identity other than taunting letters to police. Reportedly, apprehended by authorities as he attempted to kill his next victim, completely unaware he had been tailed after seen fleeing arrest by an undercover female officer earlier in the day. Prior to his execution, he confessed to an additional 30 murders in nearby Kyiv in 1934. Was convicted and executed in 1935 by firing squad at the age of 40.

title: Florisvaldo de Oliveira
text: Florisvaldo de Oliveira was a serial killer from Brazil. They were active from 1982. The proven victim count is 50, with a possible total of 50+. Known as "Cabo Bruno"; former police officer and vigilante who murdered criminals in the outskirts of São Paulo; murdered by unknown assailants in 2012.

title: Willy Pickton
text: Willy Pickton was a serial killer from Canada. They were active from 1983 to 2002. The proven victim count is 49, with a possible total of 49. Nicknamed "The Butcher"; Robert Pickton was a Canadian serial killer who killed 49 women and disposed of their bodies by feeding them to his pigs. He was convicted of only 6 murders but charged for his proven 49 victims. Unfortunately, much to the anger of the victims families, the remaining 43 charges were stayed or dropped. Sentenced to life.

title: Gary Ridgway
text: Gary Ridgway was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1982 to 2000. The proven victim count is 49, with a possible total of 71–90+. Truck painter who confessed to killing 71 women. Also known as The Green River Killer. He almost exclusively targeted sex workers from Seattle. Suspected of killing over 90 victims, confessed to 71, convicted of 49. Sentenced to life without parole.

title: Alexander Pichushkin
text: Alexander Pichushkin was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 1992 to 2006. The proven victim count is 48, with a possible total of 60. Known as the Chessboard Killer. Convicted of murdering 48 victims and suspected of killing 60. Claimed to have murdered 62 people, because he did not know that two of his victims had survived; stated goal of becoming Russia's most prolific serial killer. Sentenced to imprisonment for life.

title: Ahmad Suradji
text: Ahmad Suradji was a serial killer from Indonesia. They were active from 1986 to 1997. The proven victim count is 42, with a possible total of 70–80+. Convicted of strangling at least 42 women and girls in a series of ritual slayings he believed would give him magical powers. Executed by firing squad in 2008.

title: Raman Raghav
text: Raman Raghav was a serial killer from India. They were active from 1965 to 1968. The proven victim count is 41, with a possible total of 41. In the late 1960s Raman Raghav went on a violent rampage in Mumbai, India. He bludgeoned 41 people to death inside their huts while they slept. Died of kidney failure in 1995.

title: Moses Sithole
text: Moses Sithole was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 1994 to 1995. The proven victim count is 38, with a possible total of 76. Known as South Africa's Ted Bundy. Preyed on unemployed women, posing as a businessman and luring his victims with the prospects of a job, before leading them to an isolated place, where he raped, tortured, and murdered them. Sentenced to 2410 years imprisonment with a non-parole period of 930 years.

title: Serhiy Tkach
text: Serhiy Tkach was a serial killer from Soviet Union, Ukraine. They were active from 1984 to 2005. The proven victim count is 36, with a possible total of 80–100. A former Ukrainian police criminal investigator, suffocated girls aged between eight and 18 and performed sexual acts on their bodies after they were dead. Claims to have killed 100. Sentenced to imprisonment for life. Died from heart failure in 2018.

title: Gennady Mikhasevich
text: Gennady Mikhasevich was a serial killer from Soviet Union. They were active from 1971 to 1985. The proven victim count is 36, with a possible total of 43–55+. Strangled women. Besides killing, he also robbed his victims of money and valuable items (that he would sometimes give to his wife as a gift). Executed by firing squad in 1987.

title: Vera Renczi
text: Vera Renczi was a serial killer from Kingdom of Romania, Yugoslavia, Hungary (alleged). They were active from 1920 to 1930. The proven victim count is 35, with a possible total of ~35. Romanian serial killer nicknamed, "The Black Widow" convicted of killing 35 men through arsenic poisoning but confessed to only killing 32 victims. Renczi is one of the world's most prolific female serial killers. However, there is very little information about Renczi and her crimes because personal information (criminal history, academic records, etc.) were not cataloged as well as they are today, making some criminologists believe she was a figure of Romanian folklore rather than an actual person.

title: Ted Bundy
text: Ted Bundy was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1974 to 1978. The proven victim count is 35, with a possible total of 36–100+. American serial killer known for his charisma and good looks. Bundy officially confessed to 30 homicides, but had confessed to killing 35–36 women in the past, and some estimates run upwards of 100 or more. Infamous for escaping from prison twice and murdering multiple victims in one day; sometimes abducting women from the same location within hours of one another. He was executed by electric chair in 1989.

title: John Wayne Gacy
text: John Wayne Gacy was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1972 to 1978. The proven victim count is 33, with a possible total of 34+. Known to have murdered a minimum of 33 teenage boys and young men between 1972 and 1978, 26 of whom he buried in the crawl space of his Chicago home. Gacy was known as the "Killer Clown" due to the fact he often entertained children at social events dressed in a self devised clown costume. Executed by lethal injection in 1994.

title: Fernando Hernández Leyva
text: Fernando Hernández Leyva was a serial killer from Mexico. They were active from 1982 to 1999. The proven victim count is 33, with a possible total of 100+. Confessed to 100 murders and six kidnappings at the time of his arrest in 1999 (he had been arrested previously in 1982 and 1986, the second time for murder, but escaped from prison), but later retracted and claimed that he had been beaten by the police and his family threatened in order to force him to confess. Accused of as many as 137 murders in five southern Mexican states, convicted of 33 murders and sentenced 50 years in prison. Tried unsuccessfully to kill himself in prison. If his claim of 100+ victims were true then Leyva would be Mexico's most prolific documented serial killer.

title: Ramadan Abdel Rehim Mansour
text: Ramadan Abdel Rehim Mansour was a serial killer from Egypt. They were active from 1999 to 2006. The proven victim count is 32, with a possible total of 32+. Gang leader known as al-Tourbini ("The Express Train"). Raped and tortured homeless children, mostly boys aged 10 to 14 years old, aboard the trains between Cairo, Alexandria, Qalyubia and Beni Sueif. The victims were usually thrown off the moving train when they were dead or in agony; other times they were thrown into the Nile or buried alive. Executed in 2010.

title: Radik Tagirov
text: Radik Tagirov was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 2011 to 2012. The proven victim count is 32, with a possible total of 32. Serial killer called Radik Tagirov who murdered elderly women in several Russian regions; Kazakhstani native Pavel Shayakhmetov, found guilty of killing two elderly women in Samara, was arrested on suspicion of committing these murders [46]

title: Karl Denke
text: Karl Denke was a serial killer from Germany. They were active from 1900 to 1924. The proven victim count is 30, with a possible total of 42+. Killed and cannibalized poor travelers and homeless vagrants. Kept a ledger recording his murders with at least 31 names in it (including Vincenz Olivier, his only surviving victim), thus confirming at least 30 victims. But due to the massive amount of human remains found in his apartment, his kill count is suspected by many to exceed 42 victims. Committed suicide by hanging himself in his holding cell before he could be tried.

title: Cedric Maake
text: Cedric Maake was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 1996 to 1997. The proven victim count is 27, with a possible total of 35+. Known as the "Wemmer Pan Killer" and "Hammer Killer". He killed his victims with different instruments such as guns, rocks, a knife, and a hammer. Authorities attributed the murders to two serial killers because of the inconsistent modus operandi. In some cases he killed his victims with a rock, in others he shot them, and in others he murdered tailors with a hammer. Maake was arrested after Moses Sithole was found guilty of 38 killings and sentenced to 1,340 years in prison.

title: Mariam Soulakiotis
text: Mariam Soulakiotis was a serial killer from Greece. They were active from 1939 to 1951. The proven victim count is 27, with a possible total of 27+. Known as "The Woman Rasputin"; convent abbot believed to have murdered wealthy women and children who came into her church; the true number of her victims is a matter of debate. Her trial cites that she murdered 27 and killed an additional 150 children through neglect. Was sentenced to life and died in 1954.

title: The Stoneman
text: The Stoneman was a serial killer from India. They were active from 1985 to 1989. The proven victim count is 25, with a possible total of 25+. 12 homeless people were murdered in their sleep in Bombay between 1985 and 1987, and 13 in Calcutta in 1989—in all cases, by dropping a large rock over their head (an additional victim escaped, but could not identify the attacker). No one was ever charged with any of the murders.

title: Juan Corona
text: Juan Corona was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1971. The proven victim count is 25, with a possible total of 25+. Corona was convicted of murdering ranch laborers and burying them in orchards. He was sentenced to 25 terms life imprisonment. Died from natural causes in 2019.

title: Fritz Haarmann
text: Fritz Haarmann was a serial killer from Germany. They were active from 1918 to 1924. The proven victim count is 24, with a possible total of 27+. Also known as the Butcher of Hanover and the Vampire of Hanover, because of his preferred method of killing by biting through his victim's throat, sometimes while sodomizing them. He would then dump the bodies in the nearby river Leine. Believed to have been responsible for the murder of 27 boys and young men, he was convicted, found guilty of 24 murders and executed by guillotine in 1925.

title: Béla Kiss
text: Béla Kiss was a serial killer from Austria-Hungary. They were active from 1912 to 1916. The proven victim count is 24, with a possible total of 24+. Evaded arrest and conviction after the discovery of 24 bodies hidden in large metal drums on his property in 1916. At that time he was serving in the Austro-Hungarian Army, and deserted when the military was notified of the murders by civilian authorities. His final whereabouts and fate are unknown, as is his final victim count.

title: Majid Salek Mohammadi
text: Majid Salek Mohammadi was a serial killer from Iran. They were active from 1981 to 1985. The proven victim count is 24, with a possible total of 24. Killed mainly women he considered unfaithful to their husbands, sometimes the children accompanying them too; committed suicide before he could be sentenced.

title: Yvan Keller
text: Yvan Keller was a serial killer from France, Germany (suspected), Switzerland (suspected). They were active from 1989 to 2006. The proven victim count is 23, with a possible total of 150. Known as the "Pillow Killer"; killed and robbed old women in France's Alsace region, but also confessed to up to 150 murders, including in Germany and Switzerland; committed suicide before trial.

title: Ronald Dominique
text: Ronald Dominique was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1997 to 2006. The proven victim count is 23, with a possible total of 23+. Louisianian serial killer, known locally as the Bayou Strangler and murdered victims in the Terrebonne Parish, Lafourche Parish, Iberville Parish and Jefferson Parish. Sentenced to eight life terms.

title: Juan Fernando Hermosa
text: Juan Fernando Hermosa was a serial killer from Ecuador. They were active from 1991 to 1992. The proven victim count is 23, with a possible total of 23. Known as "Niño del Terror"; youth gang leader who murdered mostly taxi drivers and homosexuals in Quito; murdered on his 20th birthday by unknown assailants.

title: Earle Nelson
text: Earle Nelson was a serial killer from United States, Canada. They were active from 1926 to 1927. The proven victim count is 22, with a possible total of 25. Necrophiliac who primarily targeted boarding house landladies on the US West Coast during 1926; he was also known as "Gorilla Killer" or "the Dark Strangler". Captured after two murders in a small (now ghost) town in southern Manitoba. Found guilty, hanged by Canadian authorities in January 1928.

title: Mikhail Novosyolov
text: Mikhail Novosyolov was a serial killer from Soviet Union, Russia, Tajikistan. They were active from 1977 to 1995. The proven victim count is 22, with a possible total of 22. Known as the "Necrophile Rebel"; killed his victims with blows from heavy objects, then had sexual intercourse with their corpses; sentenced to civil commitment.

title: Manuel Octavio Bermúdez
text: Manuel Octavio Bermúdez was a serial killer from Colombia. They were active from 1999 to 2003. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 50+. Colombian pedophile and serial killer. Known as "El Monstruo de los Cañaduzales" (The Monster of the Cane Fields). He confessed to killing 21 children in remote areas of Colombia. Sentenced to 40 years in prison.

title: Patrick Kearney
text: Patrick Kearney was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1965 to 1977. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 43+. Would pick up young male hitch-hikers or young men from gay bars near Redondo Beach, California, and kill them. Sentenced to imprisonment for life.

title: William Bonin
text: William Bonin was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1979 to 1980. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 36+. Bonin and three accomplices are known to have murdered a minimum of 21 youths aged between 12 and 19 in and around Los Angeles. As the majority of his victims were discarded alongside various southern California freeways, Bonin became known as the Freeway Killer. Convicted of 14 of the freeway murders, he was executed by lethal injection in 1996.

title: Vasile Tcaciuc
text: Vasile Tcaciuc was a serial killer from Romania. They were active from 1917 to 1935. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 26+. Romanian man who lured victims and then murdered them with an axe. He was arrested in 1935 after a dog found a dead body in his house. He confessed to having committed at least 26 murders. He was shot dead by a policeman while trying to escape from prison.

title: Yoo Young-chul
text: Yoo Young-chul was a serial killer from South Korea. They were active from 2003 to 2004. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 26. Used a hammer to murder mostly older victims, until his focus shifted to the decapitation and mutilation of escorts after being dumped by a girlfriend who worked in that profession. Was sentenced to death and currently incarcerated.

title: Francisco Guerrero Pérez
text: Francisco Guerrero Pérez was a serial killer from Mexico. They were active from 1880 to 1908. The proven victim count is 21, with a possible total of 21. Known as "El Chaquero"; the first captured serial killer in Mexico, who killed prostitutes in Mexico City; died in 1910 from cerebral thromboembolism and another, unspecified cause.

title: José Paz Bezerra
text: José Paz Bezerra was a serial killer from Brazil. They were active from 1960s to 1970s. The proven victim count is 20, with a possible total of 20+. Known as the "Morumbi Monster"; sexually violated, tortured and then murdered at least 20 women in São Paulo and Pará; sentenced to 30 years imprisonment and later released in 2001.

title: Bulelani Mabhayi
text: Bulelani Mabhayi was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 2007 to 2012. The proven victim count is 20, with a possible total of 20. Known as the "Monster of Tholeni"; killed women and children in the village of Tholeni; sentenced from 25 years to life imprisonment.

title: Alexander Spesivtsev
text: Alexander Spesivtsev was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 1991 to 1996. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 82+. Cannibal known as "The Novokuznetsk Monster"; admitted to 19 murders, but 82 bloody sets of clothes were found in his home, along with jewels and photographs of possibly unidentified victims. Found insane and interned in a mental hospital. His mother was sentenced to 16 years in prison for luring Spesivtsev's victims to their home.

title: Larry Eyler
text: Larry Eyler was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1982 to 1984. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 23+. Known as "The Interstate Killer"; sentenced to death for the 1984 murder and dismemberment of 15-year-old Daniel Bridges. He confessed to other homicides of young men and boys in five separate states. Died of AIDS complications in 1994.

title: Surinder Koli
text: Surinder Koli was a serial killer from India. They were active from 2005 to 2006. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 30+. Between 2005 and 2006, a domestic servant of Indian businessman Moninder Singh Pandher, Surinder Koli, kidnapped, raped, murdered, and dismembered 19 people, mostly children. Convicted and sentenced to life.

title: El Psicópata
text: El Psicópata was a serial killer from Costa Rica. They were active from 1986 to 1996. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 19+. ("The Psychopath") Unidentified serial killer who killed 19 people with an M3 submachine gun in three Costa Rican towns, always south of the Florencio del Castillo Highway.

title: Sergei Ryakhovsky
text: Sergei Ryakhovsky was a serial killer from Soviet Union, Russia. They were active from 1988 to 1993. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 19+. Known as the Balashikha Ripper, he was convicted for the murders of at least 19 victims. Died from tuberculosis in 2005.

title: Yevgeny Chuplinsky
text: Yevgeny Chuplinsky was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 1998 to 2006. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 19+. Known as the "Novosibirsk Maniac"; killed prostitutes in Novosibirsk; despite extensive police search and the capture of another serial killer, he was only arrested in 2016; sentenced to life imprisonment.

title: M. Jaishankar
text: M. Jaishankar was a serial killer from India. They were active from 2008 to 2011. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 19+. Accused of killing at least 19 women. Charged with 13 murders, he escaped during a trial transport. Killed eight more people in two months before he was recaptured. Sentenced to 27 years. Committed suicide by slashing his wrists with a shaving blade in prison on 27 February 2018.

title: Vadim Ershov
text: Vadim Ershov was a serial killer from Russia. They were active from 1992 to 1995. The proven victim count is 19, with a possible total of 19. Known as the "Krasnoyarsk Beast"; committed 70 crimes around the Krasnoyarsk area, including 19 murders and eight attempted murders; sentenced to death, commuted to life imprisonment.

title: Yavuz Yapıcıoğlu
text: Yavuz Yapıcıoğlu was a serial killer from Turkey. They were active from 1994 to 2002. The proven victim count is 18, with a possible total of 40+. Killed in various cities of Turkey. 18 murders proven and accused of more than 40 by eyewitnesses and relatives. Was sentenced to 74 years in prison.

title: Christopher Mhlengwa Zikode
text: Christopher Mhlengwa Zikode was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 1994 to 1995. The proven victim count is 18, with a possible total of 18+. Known as "Donnybrook Serial Killer" murdered 18 people in Donnybrook, KwaZulu Natal from 1994 to 1995. Was given 140 years (5 life sentences).

title: Huang Yong
text: Huang Yong was a serial killer from China. They were active from 2001 to 2003. The proven victim count is 17, with a possible total of 25. Lured and murdered 17 teenage boys, although he is suspected of 25 murders between September 2001 and 2003. Executed by firing squad in 2003.

title: Pedro Pablo Nakada Ludeña
text: Pedro Pablo Nakada Ludeña was a serial killer from Peru. They were active from 2005 to 2006. The proven victim count is 17, with a possible total of 25. Known as "El Apóstol de la Muerte" ("The Apostle of Death"). Convicted of 17 murders and claimed 25. Sentenced to 35 years in prison.

title: Donato Bilancia
text: Donato Bilancia was a serial killer from Italy. They were active from 1997 to 1998. The proven victim count is 17, with a possible total of 17. Burglar who murdered 17 people, mainly prostitutes, between 1997 and 1998, during a six-month period. Known as the "Prostitutes Killer" and the "Liguria Monster". Sentenced to imprisonment for life.

title: Randy Steven Kraft
text: Randy Steven Kraft was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1971 to 1983. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 65–67. Convicted of 16 counts of murder but left a cryptic "score card" referring to at least 65 victims. May have had an accomplice. Sentenced to death.

title: Mohammed Bijeh
text: Mohammed Bijeh was a serial killer from Iran. They were active from 2004. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 20. Raped and killed at least 16 boys and teenagers. Nicknamed the "Tehran Desert Vampire". Was convicted and executed after being lashed in front of a crowd in 2005.

title: Michel Fourniret
text: Michel Fourniret was a serial killer from France, Belgium. They were active from 1987 to 2001. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 19. Known as the Ogre or Beast of Ardennes. He was caught after a failed kidnapping in 2003. Sentenced to life and died of respiratory problems while in prison in 2021.

title: Sipho Agmatir Thwala
text: Sipho Agmatir Thwala was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 1996 to 1997. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 19. Nicknamed the Phoenix Strangler after the area in which he committed his crimes; he raped and strangled 19 women; arrested, he was found guilty of 16 murders and sentenced to life.

title: Saeed Hanaei
text: Saeed Hanaei was a serial killer from Iran. They were active from 2000 to 2001. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 19. Confessed to luring 16 prostitutes to his home and killing them in an attempt to "cleanse" the city of Mashhad. His actions were dubbed "The Spider Murders". Executed by hanging in 2002.

title: Jeffrey Dahmer
text: Jeffrey Dahmer was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1978 to 1991. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 17. Dahmer ate some of his victims and kept their body parts in his freezer. Was sentenced to life imprisonment; murdered in prison in 1994.

title: José Antonio Rodríguez Vega
text: José Antonio Rodríguez Vega was a serial killer from Spain. They were active from 1987 to 1988. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 16+. Nicknamed El Mataviejas (The Old Lady Killer), he raped and killed at least 16 elderly women, aged from 61 to 93 years old, in and around Santander, Cantabria. He went unrecognized for over a year because he moved his victims into their beds after they were killed; no autopsies were made and the deaths were attributed to natural causes. He also took trophies from his victims that he held in a particular room of his home; about 10 percent of these trophies remained unclaimed, implying the existence of other victims. He was stabbed to death by two inmates while incarcerated in 2002.

title: Elias Xitavhudzi
text: Elias Xitavhudzi was a serial killer from South Africa. They were active from 1950s. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 16. Nicknamed Panga man for his use of a machete (locally known as a "panga"). He stabbed and robbed his victims between 1953 and 1959; arrested, he was executed by hanging in 1960.

title: Robert Lee Yates
text: Robert Lee Yates was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1975 to 1998. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 16. Killed prostitutes in the "Skid Row" area of E. Sprague Avenue in Spokane, Washington. Sentenced to 408 years in prison and two death sentences.

title: Charles Ray Hatcher
text: Charles Ray Hatcher was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1969 to 1982. The proven victim count is 16, with a possible total of 16. A habitual criminal, confessed to the rape and murder of over 20 young and adolescent males. Escaped from prison several times and was declared a "manipulative institutionalized sociopath". Sentenced to life; committed suicide by hanging himself in prison in 1984.

title: Lee Choon-jae
text: Lee Choon-jae was a serial killer from South Korea. They were active from 1986 to 1994. The proven victim count is 15. Also known as the Hwaseong serial murders. Most of the victims were bound, gagged, and strangled to death with their own clothes. It sparked the largest criminal case in South Korea with two million officers mobilized and over 21,000 suspects investigated. A suspect was identified in 2019, but he could not be prosecuted due to the statute of limitations. He later confessed to killing 15 people, in addition to the murder of his sister-in-law.

title: Dorángel Vargas
text: Dorángel Vargas was a serial killer from Venezuela. They were active from 1997 to 1999. The proven victim count is 15. Homeless cannibal known as "The People Eater" (El Comegente) and "The Hannibal Lecter of the Andes". Murdered a homeless man and was institutionalized. After escaping the institution, he went on to kill ten other men and was arrested. In 2016, he and several other inmates killed three others and Vargas fed their remains to them.

title: Robert Hansen
text: Robert Hansen was a serial killer from United States. They were active from 1980 to 1983. The proven victim count is 15, with a possible total of 21. Prostitutes he kidnapped were released into the Alaskan wilderness for him to hunt down like animals. Based on discovered remains, police suspect him of six murders in addition to the 15 for which he was convicted. Sentenced to 461 years plus life. Died from unspecified health problems in 2014.

title: Ángel Maturino Reséndiz
text: Ángel Maturino Reséndiz was a serial killer from United States, Mexico. They were active from 1986 to 1999. The proven victim count is 15, with a possible total of 18. Known as the "Railroad Killer" because his killings were committed near the railroad tracks he used to traverse the country. He was charged with and/or confessed to 15 murders occurring from 1986 to 1999 in Texas, Florida, Illinois, Georgia, and Kentucky. He was also suspected in a 1997 California murder case and claimed two additional killings he refused to elaborate on. Executed by lethal injection in 2006.
